fields.description:
## Why

The cross-cutting requirement is ONE shared registry consumed by all gates. The risky core is
already shared (`review_kernel/` verify/decide/coach — both gates consume it). But the
registry/descriptor layer is duplicated: plan-review has `load_criteria()` (a merged descriptor);
code-review (`b744`) has only `routing_index()` (raw routing, no descriptor loader, no
`facet/checklist`, and a **deliberately divergent** `blocking_enabled` vs `default_posture`). This story unifies them into `rebar.llm.criteria`. **b744 has LANDED on `main`** (PR #102), so
`code_review/` is available and this capstone is now workable.

## What

- Introduce `rebar.llm.criteria`: the shared merged-descriptor abstraction + loader both gates
  consume (generalize plan-review's `load_criteria`; give code-review the descriptor it lacks).
- Reconcile the **deliberate** posture divergence in one `threshold_for` that honors BOTH
  `default_posture` (plan-review) and `blocking_enabled` (code-review) — do NOT "fix" the
  divergence away.
- Exec-tier-polymorphic criteria: LLM overlays (prompt+contract+routing) AND prompt-less DET
  invariants (the 2 security keys live only in code-review's `criteria_routing.json` today).
- Route code-review's `OVERLAY_IDS` / `routing_index` / `threshold_for` through the shared
  `effective_criteria` so code-review also gets project criteria + the `.rebar/` overlay.
- **Preserve `review_code` / `code_review/shim.py`'s `review_result` return shape** — a
  back-compat surface consumed by MCP `review_code`, CLI `rebar review-code`, and the Gerrit
  `review_bot/adapter.py`.
- Docs/ADR: the how-to for authoring/activating/eval'ing/blocking on a criterion across gates.

## Scope

- Files: new `src/rebar/llm/criteria/` (shared); `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py` +
  `code_review/registry.py` (delegate to it); `code_review/` attestation port.
- Migration is expand-contract; rollback = fail-open to packaged (overlay absent ⇒ both gates
  behave as today). A misbehaving unified registry falls back per-gate.
- BLOCKED ON `b744` (epic `3156` already `depends_on b744`).

## Scope-narrowing + threshold_for design (remediation, run 1)

### Coherent single concern (clears G5 — no child decomposition)
This story is the **shared registry layer + posture reconciliation + docs** — ONE concern. The
cross-gate **attestation port** (`attest.py` → code-review + code-review `registry_version`) is
explicitly OWNED BY STORY `08af` (the attestation/invalidation story), NOT duplicated here; and
the DET-invariant consumer generalization is story `7f0d`. So the AC set below is the unification
core only (≤6 ACs, one architectural seam), not an epic-of-epics.

### `threshold_for` gate-dispatch (clears E2)
The unified `threshold_for(criteria, *, gate)` takes a **`gate` argument** (`"plan_review"` |
`"code_review"`) and applies that gate's OWN blocking convention over the SAME shared routing
records — it does NOT collapse the divergence:
- `gate="plan_review"` → a criterion blocks when its `default_posture == "blocking"`;
- `gate="code_review"` → a criterion blocks when its explicit `blocking_enabled` is true.
Both read `block_threshold` the same way (min over the criteria). Each gate's existing call site
passes its own `gate`, so neither gate's behaviour changes; the shared function just hosts both
conventions side-by-side (the deliberate posture divergence is preserved, per design).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A shared `rebar.llm.criteria` layer that both plan-review and code-review consume, replacing
      the two parallel registries.
- [ ] `threshold_for` honors BOTH posture conventions (`default_posture` + `blocking_enabled`)
      without collapsing the divergence.
- [ ] The shared descriptor is exec-tier-polymorphic (LLM overlay + prompt-less DET).
- [ ] Code-review consumes `effective_criteria` (project criteria + `.rebar/` overlay work in
      code-review too).
- [ ] `review_code` / `shim.py` `review_result` return shape is preserved (MCP + CLI + Gerrit).
- [ ] An ADR + `CLAUDE.md`/`docs/` how-to documents the unified registry across gates.
